detail:
The narrow technical core is real, but the claimed impact is materially overstated. The FA-0913/0914 fix wired redaction into _forward_to_container at poller.py:1374-1375. When a caller omits raw_user_text, line 1353 binds raw_user_text = message_text (same original string). Line 1374 rebinds message_text to its redacted result, but raw_user_text still references the ORIGINAL pre-redaction string, so line 1375 runs redact_user_message a SECOND time over identical content. Each call triggers a fresh DeBERTa NER inference pass (_detect_pii): the pipeline is loaded once via cached get_pii_pipeline(), but each pii_pipeline(text) call is fresh with no per-text memoization, so the redundant inference is genuine.

BUT it only occurs on the two MINORITY paths that omit raw_user_text: poller.py:513 (_delayed_forward after container restart) and poller.py:821 (forwarding a pending message after a 'don't update' callback). The DOMINANT inbound path, a normal Telegram message at poller.py:993, explicitly passes raw_user_text captured BEFORE decoration (line 965), so the two strings differ: message_text carries the photo prefix + _build_session_context goals/tasks/journal block + original, while raw_user_text is the bare original. On that path the two redact calls do distinct, necessary work, NOT double work. So the claim's 'roughly doubling redaction latency on the inbound hot path' and 'wasteful on every Telegram turn' are wrong, because the redundancy never hits the common turn. Redacting the large session-context block (1374) is the whole point of FA-0913/0914 (text sent to the third-party LLM must be redacted) and is necessary, not waste; the second call on the affected paths runs over the small bare excerpt anyway.

No correctness consequence (the reviewer concedes this): placeholders are reused from the tenant map, not re-minted, so no counter inflation, and redaction is fail-open. Net: a real but very low-impact inefficiency confined to two non-dominant paths, which a real operator would not notice. Genuine only on its narrow core, with the inflated impact corrected.
suggested_fix: In _forward_to_container, avoid the second NER pass when raw_user_text is the same object as message_text. Capture the original message_text before line 1374, then: if raw_user_text is the pre-redaction message_text, reuse the redacted message_text for raw_user_text instead of calling redact_user_message again. Concretely: detect aliasing before redacting (e.g. `_alias = raw_user_text is message_text` at line 1353-ish, or capture `_orig = message_text`), redact message_text at 1374, then `raw_user_text = message_text if _alias else redact_user_message(raw_user_text, tenant)`. This removes the duplicate inference on the delayed-forward and post-decline paths with no behavior change.
evidence: apps/router/poller.py:1353 raw_user_text = raw_user_text if raw_user_text is not None else message_text (aliases the two to the same string when caller omits raw_user_text)

apps/router/poller.py:1374-1375 message_text = redact_user_message(message_text, tenant); raw_user_text = redact_user_message(raw_user_text, tenant) (1374 rebinds message_text; 1375 still redacts the original, so identical content is NER'd twice on aliased paths)

apps/router/poller.py:965 + 991 + 993 dominant Telegram path passes a DISTINCT raw_user_text (bare original) vs message_text (photo prefix + session-context block + original) -> the two calls are NOT identical work on the common turn

apps/router/poller.py:513 and :821 are the only callers that omit raw_user_text (delayed-forward, post-update-decline) -> the only paths with true double work; both are minority paths, not 'every Telegram turn'
apps/pii/redactor.py:536-538 each redact_user_message ultimately calls pii_pipeline(text) (fresh DeBERTa inference, no per-text cache); pipeline object cached, inference not
apps/pii/redactor.py:384-403 placeholders reuse the tenant map / are deduped -> no counter inflation, confirming no correctness bug; redaction is fail-open (try/except returns original)

git blame: lines 1374-1375 introduced in commit c1124d2 'Phase 3 resolve 126 confirmed logistical/UX defects' (the FA-0913/0914 redaction-wiring fix); FA-1015 itself is the unrelated waking_up localization defect used only as carrier id
